summary.intent: Fix a CI failure in the moltis repo and add a pre-release workflow dispatch option to allow publishing artifacts for QA without triggering auto-update notifications to users.
summary.outcome: Fixed two sequential CI failures (invalid apt package name, then outdated Vulkan SDK version), and added a `pre_release` workflow_dispatch input to release.yml that builds/publishes artifacts but skips Homebrew tap, deploy template, and releases.json updates.

repo: Auto-update notifications are driven by `website/releases.json` being updated via the `update-deploy-template-tags` job — users poll `https://www.moltis.org/releases.json` hourly.
The LunarG Vulkan SDK repo is added in CI for Ubuntu 22.04 (jammy) — the correct package is `vulkan-sdk` (full SDK) or `glslang-tools`, not `glslc` which doesn't exist in that repo.
Ubuntu 22.04 base repos only ship libvulkan-dev 1.3.204, which is too old for llama-cpp-sys-2 v0.1.133 which needs LayerSettingsCreateInfoEXT (added in ~1.3.275+).
The release workflow already had a `dry_run` workflow_dispatch input that skips publishing but builds everything.
Vulkan shader compilation for llama-cpp-2 with the vulkan feature is a transitive build dependency — no direct references to glslc/glslang in Rust source code.

code.finding: Two jobs install build deps: the main Rust CI job and a scheduled full targets job — both needed the glslc→glslang-tools and libvulkan-dev→vulkan-sdk fixes.
Added pre_release boolean input to workflow_dispatch; when true, marks GitHub release as prerelease and skips update-homebrew-tap and update-deploy-template-tags jobs via if conditions.
Contains the update check logic that polls releases.json from the moltis.org website.

workflow: When fixing CI package issues, verify package names against the actual repo package list (e.g. LunarG Packages file) rather than assuming package names match binary names.
A two-step CI failure pattern: first fix unblocked a second deeper issue (version mismatch) that was masked by the first failure.
Pre-release workflow pattern: build and publish artifacts but skip notification/distribution channels, then promote manually after QA.

friction: A previous commit (681e9f5b) had introduced the broken `glslc` package name — the fix was effectively a revert of that change.
Had to pull/rebase before pushing on the second fix due to the first push having updated the remote.
Needed to read release.yml in multiple chunks (large file) to understand the full job dependency graph before making edits.
Security hook flagged workflow file edits as informational warnings, requiring tool retries.
open_items: After QA of the pre-release build, user needs to manually edit the GitHub release to unmark prerelease and trigger/run the deploy-tags and Homebrew tap jobs to promote to stable.
The dry-run triggered at the start (run 23498472801) was superseded — may want to cancel it if still running.
docs/src/local-validation.md was updated to reference glslang-tools — should verify the documentation accurately reflects the full install steps for local development on Ubuntu 22.04 vs 24.04.
